title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg, selected first overall by Mavericks in 2025, enjoys being with the team and doesn't want to leave.
Flagg, a Maine native and Celtics fan growing up, played his first game at TD Garden against Boston Celtics.
Despite the loss, Flagg averaged 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds as a rookie, under contract through 2030 with Dallas.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the Celtics after a nearly 10-month absence, helping secure a win against the Mavericks.
Tatum struggled initially, missing his first six shots, but finished with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's approach despite missed shots, emphasizing his positive impact on defense and rebounding.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum made a successful return to the Boston Celtics lineup after 10 months, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists.
The Celtics secured a dominant 120-100 victory over the Mavericks, aided by Jaylen Brown's 24 points and strong team performance.
Cedric Queta excelled with 16 points and 15 rebounds, benefiting from Tatum's return and forming a new pick-and-roll partnership.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins GM Don Sweeney chose not to pursue major trades at the NHL trade deadline, focusing on future growth.
The Bruins made minor AHL-level trades involving Brett Harrison, Jackson Edward, Lukas Riechel, Alexis Gendron, and Massimo Rizzo.
Sweeney maintained four first-round picks over the next two years, emphasizing a long-term rebuilding strategy for the team.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby to be traded to Baltimore Ravens for two first-round picks from Las Vegas Raiders.
Crosby expressed interest in playing for the Patriots, but New England couldn't match Baltimore's trade offer.
Patriots were unable to acquire Crosby despite having financial capacity due to his $35.8 million cap hit.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic will miss about a month due to a fractured right ring finger sustained against the Mavericks.
Vucevic was injured during a post-up effort, exited the game early, and will undergo a surgical procedure for the injury.
Vucevic has averaged 11.4 points and 8.8 rebounds since joining the Celtics, impacting the team's front-court depth.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics' lineup against the Mavericks, 297 days after his Achilles injury.
Tatum received a standing ovation at TD Garden and recorded four points, two assists, and two rebounds.
The Celtics, in second place with a 41-21 record, see Tatum's return as a boost for their playoff ambitions.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots will trade center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears for a fifth-round draft pick, per ESPN's Adam Schefter.
The trade will become official next week, creating $5.7 million cap space for the Patriots, Spotrac reports.
Drew Dalman's recent retirement motivated the Bears to acquire a new center, strengthening their offensive line.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's 'Vladimir' features Rachel Weisz as a literature professor facing a scandal involving her husband, portrayed by John Slattery.
'Hamnet', a speculative take on Shakespeare's life, is now available for streaming on Peacock.
'The Secret Agent', set in 1977 Brazil, stars Wagner Moura and is available on Hulu.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics starting lineup for the first time this season, replacing Baylor Scheierman.
Coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's work ethic and communication during his recovery following surgery.
Mazzulla noted that re-integrating Tatum into the team feels like acquiring a new player midseason.
